intro: Caliphruria candida, also known as the White Caliphruria or Candellilla, is a species of flowering plant in the yam family, Dioscoreaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Caliphruria candida include compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Caliphruria candida is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
